---
title: "random_histogram_shift"
generated: true
description: "Random monotone piecewise-linear remap of the intensity range."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.augment.intensity`](../nitrix-augment-intensity.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:random_histogram_shift -->
```python
random_histogram_shift(x: Float[Array, '...'], key: Array, *, n_control_points: int = 10, shift_range: Tuple[float, float] = (-0.1, 0.1)) -> Float[Array, '...']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:random_histogram_shift -->
Random monotone piecewise-linear remap of the intensity range.

Places `n_control_points` equally-spaced reference levels across
`[min(x), max(x)]`, perturbs each by a random offset drawn from
`shift_range` (as a fraction of the intensity span), pins the two
endpoints (so the global range is preserved), enforces monotonicity
by a running maximum, then remaps every value through the resulting
table by linear interpolation.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:random_histogram_shift -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `x` | `Float` | `...` | — | Intensity tensor. |
| `key` | `Array` | — | — | PRNG key. |
| `n_control_points` | `int` | — | `10` | Number of reference levels, including the two endpoints (`>= 2`). |
| `shift_range` | `Tuple[float, float]` | — | `(-0.1, 0.1)` | Per-control-point offset range, as a fraction of the intensity span. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `...`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:random_histogram_shift -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
